root_cause_evidence: The N1 aggregate proves HTTP 200 with no SSE error and no dataframe/SQL execution event.
The guarded run_sql tool was registered for the server and other cases executed SQL, but the actual failed LLM tool schema was not preserved.
The exact candidate SQL memory exists and passes an independent retrieval/injection gate, but actual injection into the failed HTTP request was not preserved.
The target table has identical 71-column metadata and remains the top-ranked table in both indexes.
Static inspection found no cross-request state leak; per-request IDs and history were not preserved for runtime certification.
B3R and B4 succeeded for the same question with old formal assets, but complete prompts and tool definitions are unavailable for equivalence proof.
evidence_limitations: N1 annual pH raw response.sse not found
N1 annual pH complete events.json not found
N1 final text content not found
N1 conversation_id and request_id not found
N1 and B3R raw LLM request bodies not found
N1 and B3R actual system prompts and tool-definition payloads not found
Actual per-request SQL-memory injection is not proven

excluded_classifications.CONVERSATION_OR_REQUEST_STATE_CONTAMINAT: No leak was found, but runtime IDs/history are missing.
excluded_classifications.RUN_SQL_TOOL_NOT_AVAILABLE_TO_LLM: Tool registration is proven; actual failed-request schema delivery is unknown.
excluded_classifications.CONTEXT_EQUIVALENT_MODEL_TOOL_OMISSION: The strict equivalence prerequisites are not proven.
